2604.11502.title: METER: Evaluating Multi-Level Contextual Causal Reasoning in Large Language Models
2604.11502.paper_summary: Contextual causal reasoning is a critical yet challenging capability for Large Language Models (LLMs). Existing benchmarks, however, often evaluate this skill in fragmented settings, failing to ensure context consistency or cover the full causal hierarchy. To address this, we pioneer METER to systematically benchmark LLMs across all three levels of the causal ladder under a unified context setting. Our extensive evaluation of various LLMs reveals a significant decline in proficiency as tasks ascend the causal hierarchy. To diagnose this degradation, we conduct a deep mechanistic analysis via both error pattern identification and internal information flow tracing. Our analysis reveals two primary failure modes: (1) LLMs are susceptible to distraction by causally irrelevant but factually correct information at lower level of causality; and (2) as tasks ascend the causal hierarchy, faithfulness to the provided context degrades, leading to a reduced performance. We belive our work advances our understanding of the mechanisms behind LLM contextual causal reasoning and establishes a critical foundation for future research. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/SCUNLP/METER .
2604.11502.paper_summary_zh: 上下文因果推理是大型語言模型（LLMs）一項關鍵但具挑戰性的能力。 然而，現有的基準測試通常在片段化的環境中評估這項技能，未能確保上下文的一致性或涵蓋完整的因果層級。 為了解決這個問題，我們首創了 METER，以在統一的上下文設置下系統性地基準測試 LLMs 在因果階梯的所有三個層級。 我們對各種 LLMs 的廣泛評估顯示，隨著任務在因果層級上升，熟練度顯著下降。 為了診斷這種退化，我們通過錯誤模式識別和內部信息流追踪進行了深入的機制分析。 我們的分析揭示了兩種主要的失敗模式：（1）LLMs 易受因果無關但事實上正確的信息的干擾，尤其是在較低的因果層級；（2）隨著任務在因果層級上升，對所提供上下文的忠實度下降，導致性能降低。 我們相信，我們的工作推進了對 LLM 上下文因果推理背後機制的理解，並為未來的研究奠定了重要基礎。 我們的代碼和數據集可在 https://github.com/SCUNLP/METER 獲得。
2604.11502.author: Pengfeng Li et.al.
2604.11502.authors: Pengfeng Li, Chen Huang, Chaoqun Hao, Hongyao Chen, Xiao-Yong Wei, Wenqiang Lei, See-Kiong Ng
